title: Cellular Rejuvenation Moves From Lab to First Human Trial
description: A New York Times Magazine deep dive on Altos Labs and partial epigenetic reprogramming reports that the first safety study of cellular rejuvenation has begun in humans, dosing eye tissue to test reversal of vision loss in glaucoma. Researchers have already extracted, rejuvenated, and reimplanted rat kidneys, with pig studies advancing.

title: Wedbush Calls Longevity a $120B Market After FDA Cellular Rejuvenation Clearance
description: Wedbush published a longevity-trade primer pegging the addressable market at $120B and citing FDA clearance for the first cellular rejuvenation trial as the inflection point. Longeveron's Phase 2b laromestrocel data in age-related frailty (Cell Stem Cell) is highlighted as one of the cleanest read-throughs in the public market.

title: FightAging Roundup: STING Nitrosylation, Naked Mole-Rat Microbiome, FMT Liver-Cancer Suppression
description: This week's FightAging digest spotlights three high-signal mechanisms: SNO-STING at cysteine 148 driving Alzheimer-style neuroinflammation (Scripps), young-mouse fecal microbiota transplant cutting old-mouse liver cancer to zero of eight, and the unusually stable naked mole-rat microbiome as a healthy-aging template. Each opens a new translational target distinct from the senolytic and reprogramming franchises.

title: Senolytics Pipeline Splits Into Three Lanes — Localized Wins, Systemic Doubt
description: Longevity Next's 2026 pipeline review concludes the senolytics class is bifurcating: localized indications (UNITY's DME, dermatology) keep generating clean signal while broad systemic anti-aging claims remain unproven. A 2026 Nature Aging head-to-head ranked ABT263 and ARV825 as most potent across senescent fibroblast and epithelial models, with mitochondrial quality control linked to drug resistance.

title: EIC Pathfinder Opens 2026 Healthy-Ageing Biotech Challenge
description: The European Innovation Council's Pathfinder 2026 program officially opened its biotechnology-for-healthy-ageing challenge, signaling continued non-dilutive capital flow into European geroscience. Funded teams in prior cycles have anchored several Europe-based reprogramming and senotherapeutic startups now visible in the longevity tracker.

title: Cellular Rejuvenation Has the Potential to Reverse Aging
description: Long-form profile of Altos Labs and its competitors covers rat kidney rejuvenation, planned pig studies, and the first human safety trial of partial reprogramming dosed in eye tissue for glaucoma.

title: FightAging — STING Nitrosylation Drives Alzheimer Neuroinflammation
description: Scripps team shows S-nitrosylation of STING at cysteine 148 (SNO-STING) drives chronic neuroinflammation; blocking the modification protects synapses in Alzheimer mouse models, opening a new druggable node distinct from amyloid and tau.

title: FightAging — Young-Mouse Fecal Transplant Reduces Liver Cancer in Aged Mice
description: FMT from young mice suppresses MDM2 and inflammation in old mice; in the experiment, zero of eight FMT-treated animals developed liver tumors versus two of eight controls. Microbiome-driven rejuvenation gains another preclinical data point.

title: FightAging — Naked Mole-Rat Microbiome Stable for 30+ Years
description: Multi-decade comparison shows naked mole-rats maintain a stable microbiome across decades — unlike mice — with higher diversity in pregnant queens, reinforcing microbiome resilience as a feature of exceptional-longevity species.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Combo Extends Median Mouse Lifespan 33%
description: Combination of urolithin A, quercetin, NR, ALA, and SRN-820 increased median lifespan by 33% in old mice and reduced frailty. Provides translational design template for multi-target geroprotector stacks now common in protocol papers.

title: Inflammation's Statin Moment Edges Closer
description: Editorial sweep of NLRP3 inhibitors, IL-33 antibodies, and IL-6 antagonists frames inflammaging as on the cusp of a statin-style mass-market repositioning, citing BioAge BGE-102 and AstraZeneca tozorakimab as bellwether assets.

title: New Senolytic Strategy Targets a Key Vulnerability in Ageing Cells
description: Imperial researchers identified GPX4-targeting chloroacetamides that selectively kill senescent cells via ferroptosis. Combination with cancer therapy cleared persistent senescent tumor cells in melanoma, prostate, and ovarian models (Nature Cell Biology).

title: Cells That Heal? New Parkinson's Trial Sparks Hope
description: Longevity.Technology covers a new Parkinson's cell-therapy trial leveraging engineered cell delivery and tissue-targeting strategies relevant to broader rejuvenation programs.

title: Mesoblast Advances CAR-MSC Platform; 300-Patient Phase 3 Enrolled
description: Mesoblast (ASX:MSB) finished enrolling its 300-patient Phase 3 of rexlemestrocel-L in chronic low back pain and licensed Mayo Clinic CAR technology to upgrade MSC homing for IBD and CD19-targeted lupus nephritis programs.

title: INBRAIN Completes First-in-Human Graphene BCI Enrollment
description: INBRAIN Neuroelectronics finished recruiting its graphene cortical interface study, with 8 of 10 patients implanted and no device-related adverse events to date. Final results expected later in 2026.

title: Sinclair: Human Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial Days Away
description: At Abundance360, David Sinclair said the first human partial-reprogramming trial using three Yamanaka factors will dose a patient's eye to test reversal of vision loss within weeks, framing 2026 as the year age reversal moves from speculation to early clinical signal.

title: Anti-Ageing Tech: Newest 2026 Fixes (Liv Hospital Roundup)
description: Roundup of where stem-cell, gene-therapy, and AI-driven longevity tech stand mid-2026, including Genflow's SIRT6 program and AI-generated lifespan compounds reported at 70% hit rates.

title: Longevity Biotech Hits $3.74B in Q1 2026 Financing
description: Longevity.Technology's DLT tracker recorded $3.74B raised across 49 financings in Q1 2026, up 56% year-over-year, concentrated in epigenetic reprogramming and mitochondrial health.

title: Wedbush — Longevity Trade is a $120B Market
description: Wedbush analyst note frames longevity as a $120B market following FDA clearance of the first cellular rejuvenation trial and Longeveron's positive Phase 2b laromestrocel data in age-related frailty.

title: Energy Span Reframes Fatigue as Early Aging Warning
description: New 'energy span' biomarker frames fatigue as an early window into mitochondrial decline, supplementing healthspan and lifespan as patient-facing metrics.

title: Longevity Tracker — 13 Companies, $792.9M Across 12 Months
description: Pure-play longevity equity tracker shows 14 disclosed deals from 13 companies summing to $792.9M; five rounds above $50M captured 81% of dollars.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026 Berlin — Strategies to Target Aging
description: Recap of the 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity highlights partial reprogramming, organ-specific proteomic clocks, and AI-designed proteins as 2026's most promising vectors.

title: Rapamycin and Exercise: New Trial Finds Performance Decline
description: RAPA-EX-01 in sedentary older adults (6 mg/week sirolimus) found smaller red cells, more platelets, higher alkaline phosphatase, higher HbA1c, and higher LDL versus placebo — clinically modest but a useful counterweight to rapamycin enthusiasm.

title: Insilico Forms Industry-First Longevity Board
description: HKEX-listed Insilico Medicine (3696) launched the industry's first Longevity Board to oversee aging-research strategy across life models, biomarkers, and target identification.

title: BioAge BGE-102 Phase 1 Hits Best-in-Class hsCRP Reductions
description: Brain-penetrant oral NLRP3 inhibitor showed potential best-in-class hsCRP reductions in obese participants with no serious adverse events; Phase 2 cardiovascular-risk POC due H2 2026.

title: AstraZeneca Tozorakimab Wins Third Phase 3 COPD Trial
description: MIRANDA (NCT06040086) showed statistically significant reductions in moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations on top of standard inhaled care, completing a clean three-trial Phase 3 sweep for the IL-33 antibody.

title: Tozorakimab Phase 3 Sweep Reignites Anti-IL-33 Class
description: AZ's three-for-three Phase 3 COPD wins (OBERON, TITANIA, MIRANDA) revive an IL-33 modality that previously stumbled with Sanofi/Regeneron's itepekimab and Roche's astegolimab.

title: University of Arizona Launches Largest-Ever Human Rapamycin Trial
description: 720-participant Phase 3 of weekly low-dose rapamycin runs two years dosing plus a third year follow-up, with co-primary endpoints of frailty transition and IL-6 plus epigenetic-clock biomarker panel.

title: Bryan Johnson on Biological Age and Healthy Code
description: At BI's Long Play event, Johnson emphasized resting heart rate as an underrated longevity metric and warned founders against monk-mode burnout undermining healthspan.

title: Peter Attia: Research Worth Sharing — April 2026 Edition
description: Curated set covering paternal exercise reshaping offspring fitness via sperm microRNAs, mRNA COVID vaccines boosting cancer immunotherapy, gamma light/sound stimulation for Alzheimer's, and autonomic imbalance as a unifying aging theory.
